/home/nick/work/libnn/test.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#
# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
# performance.
#
# Input:
#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
#       of libdsp.
#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
#       environment variable before using this script.
#   $3: Simulator name
#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
#   $4: Simulation target
#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
#              will be verified in this mode.
#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
#       used to build the library.
#   $7: Specified linker
#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
#
# Example:
#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
#
#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
#
#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
#
#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
#
#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
#
#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
#
# Note:
#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
#   be used in the accuray test later.
#
################################################################################

#######################################################
# check the coverage of executed functions
#######################################################
function check_coverage {
    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    cd ${GCOV_DIR}

    gcda_list=($(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gcda))
    for gcda in ${gcda_list[@]}; do
        called_fun_list=$(${GCOV} -f ${gcda} | grep -w "Function" -A1)
        IFS=-
        called_fun_list=(${called_fun_list})
        IFS=
        coverage_status=""
        is_coverage_fail=0

        # let the function with riscv_nn prefix be shown first
        for called_fun in "${called_fun_list[@]}"; do
            if [[ "${called_fun}" == *"${FUN_PREFIX}"* ]]; then
                fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
                # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
                    fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
                    coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
                    # coverage_status+=""
                    if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
                        is_coverage_fail=1
                    fi
                fi

                called_fun_list=("${called_fun_list[@]/${called_fun}}")

content.text: The program hit an exception — likely because the binary needs different setup for FPGA (no file I/O). Let me use `test.sh` properly but only run the single test. First let me set up run.sh to only run our target:

content.content: The file /home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/unit_func/run.sh has been updated successfully.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
        # the test program is executed.
        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            fail_list+=("${1}")
            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
        else
            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
        fi

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            #dump inst/cycle into csv
            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
        fi
    else
        fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

################################################################################
#                                   main                                       #
################################################################################
ENA_TEST_ALL=1
ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
ENA_BASIC_TEST=0
ENA_CONCATE_TEST=0
ENA_CONV_TEST=0
ENA_FC_TEST=0
ENA_POOLING_TEST=0
ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=0
ENA_UTIL_TEST=0

ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0           # only for the test programs with DYNAMIC allocation buffers
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # only run large test case to measure the performance (
                            # run the program with DYNAMIC allocation buffers)
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep instruction and cycle counts into csv
ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=0   # run the test prgram with STATIC allocation buffers
ENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0          # The testers can turn on this switch as running the
                            # test programs in the environment not supporting
                            # FILE I/O, e.g. m4 or m7. The inputs will be randomly
                            # generated and the results will not be verified.
                            # (this switch is ONLY for STATIC allocation buffer tests)
ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER=0   # verify the wrapper header file between andes-nn and cmsis-nn

# Since strided load is not supported now. If vector extension
# is enabled, those functions use strided load won't be executed.
# if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mvh"* ]]; then
#     ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=1      #only run the test cases with unit stride acess
# else
    ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=0
# fi

#===== configure test environment_begin =====
CC=${1}
if [[ $CC =~ - ]]; then
    OBJDUMP="${CC%-*}-objdump"
else
    OBJDUMP="objdump"
fi
SIM_WRAPPER=${2}
EXTRA_FLAG="${3}"
CFLAGS="-Wall -Werror -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
MACRO="-DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=${ENA_RANDOM_INPUT}"
UNIT_TEST_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../unit_performance"
INC_DIR="-I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../Include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm-project/libhvm/include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include/"
LINKED_LIB="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libnn.a"
if [ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC} -eq 1 ]; then
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_pf_"
else    #ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_"
fi
if [ ${ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER} -eq 1 ]; then
    INC_DIR+=" -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../cmsis_include"
    MACRO+=" -DENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER"
fi
if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
    # As using the v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default for
    # some floating-piont functions.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO"

    # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
    # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
fi
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
    LINKED_LIB+=" ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm.a"
fi
#===== configure test environment_end =====

# create folders and clean old files
mkdir -p ${ADX_DIR}
mkdir -p ${OBJDUMP_DIR}
mkdir -p ${LOG_DIR}
rm -f ${LOG_DIR}/*.log          # clean old logs
rm -f ${ADX_DIR}/*.adx          # clean old executables
rm -f ${OBJDUMP_DIR}/*.objdump  # clean old objdump files
#init global variable
pass_num=0
unsupport_num=0

ACTIVATION=(
    "activate_s8"
    "activate_s16"
    "activate_s16_hp"
    "hardswish_s8"
    "leaky_relu_s8"
    "leaky_relu_s8_asym"
    "leaky_relu_s16_asym"
    "prelu_s8_asym"
    "relu_s8"
    "relu_s16"
    "relu_any_s8"
    "relu_any_s16"
    "sigmoid_s8"
    "sigmoid_s16"
    "silu_s8"
    "tanh_s8"
    "tanh_s16"

    "activate_s8_2buf"
    "activate_s16_2buf"
    "leaky_relu_s8_2buf"
    "relu_s8_2buf"
    "relu_s16_2buf"
    "relu_any_s8_2buf"

    "fc_mat_vec_s16_s16_s8_sft_bias_fast"

if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${ACTIVATION[@]/%/:activation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_BASIC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${BASIC[@]/%/:basic}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONCATE_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONCATE[@]/%/:concatenation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONV_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONV[@]/%/:convolution}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_FC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${FC[@]/%/:fully_connected}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_POOLING_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${POOLING[@]/%/:pooling}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${SOFTMAX[@]/%/:softmax}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_UTIL_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${UTIL[@]/%/:util}")
fi

#remove non-unit-stride test cases
if [ ${ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY} == 1 ]; then
    for FUN_NAME in "${NON_UNIT_STRIDE[@]}";
    do
        test_list=(${test_list[@]/$FUN_NAME})
    done
fi

# record whether the zfh extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} == *"-mzfh"* ]]; then
    ENA_FP16=1
else
    ENA_FP16=0
fi

# record whether the VL4 extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_VL4 ]]; then
    ENA_VL4=1
else
    ENA_VL4=0
fi

if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    #init csv title
    echo "group, function, inst, cycle" > ${CSV_OUT}
fi

#----- enable the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

for item in "${test_list[@]}"; do
    fun_name=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f1)
    fun_group=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f2)
    run_and_check "${fun_name}" "${fun_group}"
done

#----- restore the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="lm"/CACHE="on"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

# only show the execution summary as ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN is disabled
if [ ${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -eq 0 ]; then
    #show result summary
    total_num=${#test_list[@]}
    executed_num="$((total_num - unsupport_num))"
    printf "\n========== accuracy checking summary ==========\n"
    printf "Total functions:       %4d\n" "${#test_list[@]}"
    printf "Executed functions:    %4d\n" "${executed_num}"
    printf "Passed functions:      %4d (pass rate: %.2f%%)\n" "${pass_num}" "$(echo ${pass_num}*100/${executed_num} | bc -l)"
    printf "Unsupported functions: %4d\n" "${unsupport_num}"
fi

# show fail list
if [ ${#fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
    echo
    echo "failed funcion list:"
    for FUN_NAME in "${fail_list[@]}"; do
        echo ${FUN_NAME}
    done
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
        # the test program is executed.
        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            fail_list+=("${1}")
            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
        else
            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
        fi

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            #dump inst/cycle into csv
            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
        fi
    else
        fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

################################################################################
#                                   main                                       #
################################################################################
ENA_TEST_ALL=0
ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
ENA_BASIC_TEST=0
ENA_CONCATE_TEST=0
ENA_CONV_TEST=1
ENA_FC_TEST=0
ENA_POOLING_TEST=0
ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=0
ENA_UTIL_TEST=0

ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0           # only for the test programs with DYNAMIC allocation buffers
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # only run large test case to measure the performance (
                            # run the program with DYNAMIC allocation buffers)
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep instruction and cycle counts into csv
ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=0   # run the test prgram with STATIC allocation buffers
ENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0          # The testers can turn on this switch as running the
                            # test programs in the environment not supporting
                            # FILE I/O, e.g. m4 or m7. The inputs will be randomly
                            # generated and the results will not be verified.
                            # (this switch is ONLY for STATIC allocation buffer tests)
ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER=0   # verify the wrapper header file between andes-nn and cmsis-nn

# Since strided load is not supported now. If vector extension
# is enabled, those functions use strided load won't be executed.
# if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mvh"* ]]; then
#     ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=1      #only run the test cases with unit stride acess
# else
    ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=0
# fi

#===== configure test environment_begin =====
CC=${1}
if [[ $CC =~ - ]]; then
    OBJDUMP="${CC%-*}-objdump"
else
    OBJDUMP="objdump"
fi
SIM_WRAPPER=${2}
EXTRA_FLAG="${3}"
CFLAGS="-Wall -Werror -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
MACRO="-DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=${ENA_RANDOM_INPUT}"
UNIT_TEST_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../unit_performance"
INC_DIR="-I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../Include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm-project/libhvm/include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include/"
LINKED_LIB="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libnn.a"
if [ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC} -eq 1 ]; then
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_pf_"
else    #ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_"
fi
if [ ${ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER} -eq 1 ]; then
    INC_DIR+=" -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../cmsis_include"
    MACRO+=" -DENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER"
fi
if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
    # As using the v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default for
    # some floating-piont functions.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO"

    # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
    # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
fi
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
    LINKED_LIB+=" ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm.a"
fi
#===== configure test environment_end =====

# create folders and clean old files
mkdir -p ${ADX_DIR}
mkdir -p ${OBJDUMP_DIR}
mkdir -p ${LOG_DIR}
rm -f ${LOG_DIR}/*.log          # clean old logs
rm -f ${ADX_DIR}/*.adx          # clean old executables
rm -f ${OBJDUMP_DIR}/*.objdump  # clean old objdump files
#init global variable
pass_num=0

ACTIVATION=(

if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${ACTIVATION[@]/%/:activation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_BASIC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${BASIC[@]/%/:basic}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONCATE_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONCATE[@]/%/:concatenation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONV_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONV[@]/%/:convolution}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_FC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${FC[@]/%/:fully_connected}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_POOLING_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${POOLING[@]/%/:pooling}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${SOFTMAX[@]/%/:softmax}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_UTIL_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${UTIL[@]/%/:util}")
fi

#remove non-unit-stride test cases
if [ ${ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY} == 1 ]; then
    for FUN_NAME in "${NON_UNIT_STRIDE[@]}";
    do
        test_list=(${test_list[@]/$FUN_NAME})
    done
fi

# record whether the zfh extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} == *"-mzfh"* ]]; then
    ENA_FP16=1
else
    ENA_FP16=0
fi

# record whether the VL4 extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_VL4 ]]; then
    ENA_VL4=1
else
    ENA_VL4=0
fi

if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    #init csv title
    echo "group, function, inst, cycle" > ${CSV_OUT}
fi

#----- enable the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

for item in "${test_list[@]}"; do
    fun_name=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f1)
    fun_group=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f2)
    run_and_check "${fun_name}" "${fun_group}"
done

#----- restore the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="lm"/CACHE="on"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

# only show the execution summary as ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN is disabled
if [ ${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -eq 0 ]; then
    #show result summary
    total_num=${#test_list[@]}
    executed_num="$((total_num - unsupport_num))"
    printf "\n========== accuracy checking summary ==========\n"
    printf "Total functions:       %4d\n" "${#test_list[@]}"
    printf "Executed functions:    %4d\n" "${executed_num}"
    printf "Passed functions:      %4d (pass rate: %.2f%%)\n" "${pass_num}" "$(echo ${pass_num}*100/${executed_num} | bc -l)"
    printf "Unsupported functions: %4d\n" "${unsupport_num}"
fi

# show fail list
if [ ${#fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
    echo
    echo "failed funcion list:"
    for FUN_NAME in "${fail_list[@]}"; do
        echo ${FUN_NAME}
    done
fi

I was about to run `test.sh` which rebuilds the entire library from scratch before running the test — wasteful since `libnn.a` is already built with our changes.

The smarter approach: just use the board wrapper directly with a properly compiled binary. The earlier FPGA attempt failed because my binary tried to do file I/O (ecall exception). For FPGA, the test needs `ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1` which uses statically-embedded data instead of file reads.

Want me to just compile the single test binary with the correct FPGA flags and run it via the board wrapper on port 1117?

content.text:
The user is saying the `-mvh` flag enables semihosting I/O output on the board. Looking at the GDB output — it **does** see file names and prints errors, so semihosting I/O is partially working. The issue is that `fopen("8b_in1.bin")` returns NULL even though the files exist in the CWD.

Wait — the GDB semihosting resolves file paths relative to the **GDB process CWD**, but the expect script may be spawned from a different directory. Let me check what the `test.sh` baseline did differently. The baseline worked from `build_perf_ax45mpv/test_sh_*` with a `-DGETENV_TESTDIR` flag.

content.content:

content.text: That's the key. When `GETENV_TESTDIR` is defined, it prepends the full path to `bin/`. Without it, it assumes `bin/` is relative to CWD. Let me recompile with `-DGETENV_TESTDIR=/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/unit_func`:

input.command:

export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com && \
export MYPORT=1116 && \
\
riscv64-elf-gcc \
  -Wall -Werror -O3 -mvh \
  -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 \
  -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER \
  -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mzfh \
  -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE \
  -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1 -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0 \
  -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP \
  -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_VL4 \
  -DGETENV_TESTDIR=/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/unit_func \
  -I../../Include/ -I../../libhvm-project/libhvm/include/ -I../../Examples/unit_func/include/ \
  -o t_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any.adx \

echo "=== Compile OK ===" && \

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
        # the test program is executed.
        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            fail_list+=("${1}")
            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
        else
            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
        fi

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            #dump inst/cycle into csv
            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
        fi
    else
        fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

################################################################################
#                                   main                                       #
################################################################################
ENA_TEST_ALL=0
ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
ENA_BASIC_TEST=0
ENA_CONCATE_TEST=0
ENA_CONV_TEST=1
ENA_FC_TEST=0
ENA_POOLING_TEST=0
ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=0
ENA_UTIL_TEST=0

ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0           # only for the test programs with DYNAMIC allocation buffers
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # only run large test case to measure the performance (
                            # run the program with DYNAMIC allocation buffers)
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep instruction and cycle counts into csv
ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=0   # run the test prgram with STATIC allocation buffers
ENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0          # The testers can turn on this switch as running the
                            # test programs in the environment not supporting
                            # FILE I/O, e.g. m4 or m7. The inputs will be randomly
                            # generated and the results will not be verified.
                            # (this switch is ONLY for STATIC allocation buffer tests)
ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER=0   # verify the wrapper header file between andes-nn and cmsis-nn

# Since strided load is not supported now. If vector extension
# is enabled, those functions use strided load won't be executed.
# if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mvh"* ]]; then
#     ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=1      #only run the test cases with unit stride acess
# else
    ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=0
# fi

#===== configure test environment_begin =====
CC=${1}
if [[ $CC =~ - ]]; then
    OBJDUMP="${CC%-*}-objdump"
else
    OBJDUMP="objdump"
fi
SIM_WRAPPER=${2}
EXTRA_FLAG="${3}"
CFLAGS="-Wall -Werror -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
MACRO="-DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=${ENA_RANDOM_INPUT}"
UNIT_TEST_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../unit_performance"
INC_DIR="-I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../Include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm-project/libhvm/include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include/"
LINKED_LIB="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libnn.a"
if [ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC} -eq 1 ]; then
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_pf_"
else    #ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_"
fi
if [ ${ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER} -eq 1 ]; then
    INC_DIR+=" -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../cmsis_include"
    MACRO+=" -DENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER"
fi
if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
    # As using the v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default for
    # some floating-piont functions.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO"

    # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
    # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
fi
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
    LINKED_LIB+=" ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm.a"
fi
#===== configure test environment_end =====

# create folders and clean old files
mkdir -p ${ADX_DIR}
mkdir -p ${OBJDUMP_DIR}
mkdir -p ${LOG_DIR}
rm -f ${LOG_DIR}/*.log          # clean old logs
rm -f ${ADX_DIR}/*.adx          # clean old executables
rm -f ${OBJDUMP_DIR}/*.objdump  # clean old objdump files
#init global variable
pass_num=0
unsupport_num=0

ACTIVATION=(
    "activate_s8"
    "activate_s16"
    "activate_s16_hp"
    "hardswish_s8"
    "leaky_relu_s8"
    "leaky_relu_s8_asym"
    "leaky_relu_s16_asym"
    "prelu_s8_asym"
    "relu_s8"
    "relu_s16"
    "relu_any_s8"
    "relu_any_s16"
    "sigmoid_s8"
    "sigmoid_s16"
    "silu_s8"
    "tanh_s8"
    "tanh_s16"

    "activate_s8_2buf"
    "activate_s16_2buf"
    "leaky_relu_s8_2buf"
    "relu_s8_2buf"
    "relu_s16_2buf"
    "relu_any_s8_2buf"

    "gelu_f16"
    "gelu_f32"
    "leaky_relu_f16"
    "relu_f16"

if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_BASIC_TEST} == 1 ]; then

fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONCATE_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONCATE[@]/%/:concatenation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONV_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONV[@]/%/:convolution}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_FC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${FC[@]/%/:fully_connected}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_POOLING_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${POOLING[@]/%/:pooling}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${SOFTMAX[@]/%/:softmax}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_UTIL_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${UTIL[@]/%/:util}")
fi

#remove non-unit-stride test cases

    for FUN_NAME in "${NON_UNIT_STRIDE[@]}";
    do
        test_list=(${test_list[@]/$FUN_NAME})
    done
fi

# record whether the zfh extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} == *"-mzfh"* ]]; then
    ENA_FP16=1
else
    ENA_FP16=0
fi

# record whether the VL4 extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_VL4 ]]; then
    ENA_VL4=1
else
    ENA_VL4=0
fi

if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    #init csv title
    echo "group, function, inst, cycle" > ${CSV_OUT}
fi

#----- enable the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

for item in "${test_list[@]}"; do
    fun_name=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f1)
    fun_group=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f2)
    run_and_check "${fun_name}" "${fun_group}"
done

#----- restore the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="lm"/CACHE="on"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

# only show the execution summary as ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN is disabled
if [ ${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -eq 0 ]; then
    #show result summary
    total_num=${#test_list[@]}
    executed_num="$((total_num - unsupport_num))"
    printf "\n========== accuracy checking summary ==========\n"
    printf "Total functions:       %4d\n" "${#test_list[@]}"
    printf "Executed functions:    %4d\n" "${executed_num}"
    printf "Passed functions:      %4d (pass rate: %.2f%%)\n" "${pass_num}" "$(echo ${pass_num}*100/${executed_num} | bc -l)"
    printf "Unsupported functions: %4d\n" "${unsupport_num}"
fi

# show fail list
if [ ${#fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
    echo
    echo "failed funcion list:"
    for FUN_NAME in "${fail_list[@]}"; do
        echo ${FUN_NAME}
    done
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
        # the test program is executed.
        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            fail_list+=("${1}")
            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
        else
            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
        fi

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            #dump inst/cycle into csv
            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
        fi
    else
        fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

#                                   main                                       #
################################################################################
ENA_TEST_ALL=1
ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
ENA_BASIC_TEST=0

                            # run the program with DYNAMIC allocation buffers)
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep instruction and cycle counts into csv
ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=0   # run the test prgram with STATIC allocation buffers

                            # test programs in the environment not supporting

                            # generated and the results will not be verified.

MACRO="-DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=${ENA_RANDOM_INPUT}"
UNIT_TEST_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../unit_performance"
INC_DIR="-I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../Include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm-project/libhvm/include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include/"
LINKED_LIB="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libnn.a"

    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_pf_"
else    #ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_"
fi
if [ ${ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER} -eq 1 ]; then
    INC_DIR+=" -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../cmsis_include"
    MACRO+=" -DENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER"
fi
if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
    # As using the v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default for
    # some floating-piont functions.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO"

    # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
    # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
fi
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
    LINKED_LIB+=" ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm.a"
fi
#===== configure test environment_end =====

# create folders and clean old files
mkdir -p ${ADX_DIR}
mkdir -p ${OBJDUMP_DIR}
mkdir -p ${LOG_DIR}
rm -f ${LOG_DIR}/*.log          # clean old logs
rm -f ${ADX_DIR}/*.adx          # clean old executables
rm -f ${OBJDUMP_DIR}/*.objdump  # clean old objdump files
#init global variable
pass_num=0
unsupport_num=0

if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONCATE_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONCATE[@]/%/:concatenation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONV_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONV[@]/%/:convolution}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_FC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${FC[@]/%/:fully_connected}")

if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_POOLING_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${POOLING[@]/%/:pooling}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${SOFTMAX[@]/%/:softmax}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_UTIL_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${UTIL[@]/%/:util}")
fi

#remove non-unit-stride test cases

    done

# record whether the zfh extension is enabled

else

fi

# record whether the VL4 extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_VL4 ]]; then
    ENA_VL4=1
else
    ENA_VL4=0
fi

if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    #init csv title
    echo "group, function, inst, cycle" > ${CSV_OUT}
fi

#----- enable the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

for item in "${test_list[@]}"; do
    fun_name=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f1)
    fun_group=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f2)
    run_and_check "${fun_name}" "${fun_group}"
done

#----- restore the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="lm"/CACHE="on"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

# only show the execution summary as ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN is disabled
if [ ${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -eq 0 ]; then
    #show result summary
    total_num=${#test_list[@]}
    executed_num="$((total_num - unsupport_num))"
    printf "\n========== accuracy checking summary ==========\n"
    printf "Total functions:       %4d\n" "${#test_list[@]}"
    printf "Executed functions:    %4d\n" "${executed_num}"
    printf "Passed functions:      %4d (pass rate: %.2f%%)\n" "${pass_num}" "$(echo ${pass_num}*100/${executed_num} | bc -l)"
    printf "Unsupported functions: %4d\n" "${unsupport_num}"
fi

# show fail list
if [ ${#fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
    echo
    echo "failed funcion list:"
    for FUN_NAME in "${fail_list[@]}"; do
        echo ${FUN_NAME}
    done
fi

run.sh restored. Here's the final result:

## Optimization Result: `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any`

| Metric | Baseline | Optimized | Improvement |
|--------|----------|-----------|-------------|
| Instructions | 3,826,922 | 1,039,912 | **3.68x fewer** |
| Cycles | 5,455,029 | 2,187,287 | **2.49x faster** |
| IPC | 0.70 | 0.48 | (expected — vd4dots has longer latency) |
| Accuracy | pass | pass | correct |

**2.49x speedup** by dispatching to `nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2` (vd4dots path) when `out_tensor_ch <= 64`, vectorizing along the reduction dimension instead of the small output channel dimension.

The baseline is from the previous FPGA run saved at `build_perf_ax45mpv/test_sh_20260320150527/performance.csv` — the **unmodified code** running the same test case on the same FPGA board.

But that run used the full `test.sh` pipeline which may have different compile flags or library build options than my manual compile. To get a fair apples-to-apples comparison, I should run the **unmodified code** with the exact same manual compile flags I just used.

Want me to revert the code change, rebuild, run the same single test on FPGA, then re-apply the optimization and compare?

Both runs on the same board, same flags, back-to-back:

## Apples-to-apples FPGA comparison

| | Baseline | Optimized | Speedup |
|--|----------|-----------|---------|
| **Instructions** | 3,826,922 | 1,039,912 | **3.68x** |
| **Cycles** | 5,456,391 | 2,187,394 | **2.49x** |
| **Accuracy** | pass | pass | |

**2.49x cycle reduction** on the AX45MPV FPGA for `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any` (test-3: in=32x30x64, ker=3x3, out=30x28x16, batch=2).

message.content: Now, let's review what you have solved. First, QEMU and FPGA runner, write into two scripts to ensure smooth running next time. (remember to parameterize )

content.text:
Let me first review the key lessons learned, then write both scripts.

**QEMU issues solved:**
- Output goes to fd 9 (not stdout) in this environment — use `strace -e write` to capture
- Must have `-mvh` for semihosting
- CWD doesn't matter if golden data path is embedded

**FPGA issues solved:**
- Need `-DGETENV_TESTDIR=<path>` so binary finds `bin/*.bin` golden data via absolute path
- Need `-mvh` for GDB semihosting file I/O  
- Must `rm -f First_Reset_done` before first run
- Board port matters — parameterize it

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# QEMU Validation Script for libnn
#
# Builds libnn, compiles a single unit test, and runs it on QEMU to verify
# correctness against golden data.
#
# Usage:
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> [options]
#
# Examples:
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --vlen 1024
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --skip-build
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace
#
# Environment:
#   TOOLCHAIN_PATH  - Path to Andes toolchain bin/ (default: /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin)
#   QEMU_SYS_RV64   - Path to QEMU binary (default: /local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64)
################################################################################
set -euo pipefail

#===== Defaults =====
TOOLCHAIN_PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH:-/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin}"
QEMU_SYS_RV64="${QEMU_SYS_RV64:-/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VLEN=512
SKIP_BUILD=0
USE_STRACE=0
TIMEOUT=120
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
VEC_CFG="zve64d"

#===== Parse arguments =====
TEST_NAME="${1:-}"
if [ -z "${TEST_NAME}" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <test_name> [--vlen N] [--skip-build] [--strace] [--timeout N] [--cpu NAME]"
    echo ""
    echo "  test_name     Unit test name (without t_ prefix), e.g. conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any"
    echo "  --vlen N      Vector length: 128, 256, 512 (default), 1024"
    echo "  --skip-build  Skip library rebuild (use existing libnn.a)"
    echo "  --strace      Use strace to capture output (workaround for fd redirection issues)"
    echo "  --timeout N   QEMU timeout in seconds (default: 120)"
    echo "  --cpu NAME    CPU build target (default: ax45mpv)"
    exit 1
fi
shift

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --vlen)    VLEN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --skip-build) SKIP_BUILD=1; shift ;;
        --strace)  USE_STRACE=1; shift ;;
        --timeout) TIMEOUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cpu)     CPU_NAME="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        *) echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

#===== Resolve paths =====
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
PROJECT_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv"
UNIT_TEST_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
WORK_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/work"

export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}:${LIB_ROOT}:${PATH}"
CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"

# Select QEMU wrapper based on VLEN
QEMU_WRAPPER="${PROJECT_DIR}/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v${VLEN}_d${VLEN}_b512"
if [ ! -f "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" ]; then
    # Try common fallback patterns
    QEMU_WRAPPER="${PROJECT_DIR}/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v${VLEN}_d$((VLEN/2))_b512"
fi
if [ ! -f "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" ]; then
    echo "Error: No QEMU wrapper found for VLEN=${VLEN} in ${PROJECT_DIR}"
    ls "${PROJECT_DIR}"/riscv-qemu-wrapper-* 2>/dev/null
    exit 1
fi

echo "============================================"
echo "  QEMU Validation: t_${TEST_NAME}"
echo "============================================"
echo "  VLEN:       ${VLEN}"
echo "  CPU:        ${CPU_NAME}"
echo "  Toolchain:  ${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}"
echo "  QEMU:       ${QEMU_SYS_RV64}"
echo "  Wrapper:    $(basename ${QEMU_WRAPPER})"
echo "  Strace:     ${USE_STRACE}"
echo "============================================"

#===== Step 1: Build library =====
if [ ${SKIP_BUILD} -eq 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "[1/3] Building libnn for ${CPU_NAME}..."
    LIB_CFLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER -fno-strict-aliasing -I./Include -I./internal -mzfh"
    cd "${LIB_ROOT}"
    bash build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}" > /dev/null 2>&1
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "  FAILED: Library build error"
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "  OK: libnn.a built"
else
    echo ""
    echo "[1/3] Skipping library build (--skip-build)"
    if [ ! -f "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" ]; then
        echo "  ERROR: libnn.a not found. Remove --skip-build."
        exit 1
    fi
fi

#===== Step 2: Compile test binary =====
echo ""
echo "[2/3] Compiling test binary..."
mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"

TEST_SRC="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.c"
TEST_ADX="${WORK_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.adx"

if [ ! -f "${TEST_SRC}" ]; then
    echo "  ERROR: Test source not found: ${TEST_SRC}"
    exit 1
fi

${CC} -mvh \
    -Wall -Werror -O3 \
    -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 \
    -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER \
    -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mzfh \
    -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE \
    -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=0 -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0 \
    -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -DENA_VL4 \
    -I"${LIB_ROOT}/Include/" \
    -I"${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include/" \
    -I"${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/include/" \
    -o "${TEST_ADX}" \
    "${TEST_SRC}" \
    "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" -lm 2>&1

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "  FAILED: Compilation error"
    exit 1
fi
echo "  OK: $(basename ${TEST_ADX})"

#===== Step 3: Run on QEMU =====
echo ""
echo "[3/3] Running on QEMU (timeout=${TIMEOUT}s)..."

export QEMU_SYS_RV64
export VEC_CFG

cd "${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"

if [ ${USE_STRACE} -eq 1 ]; then
    # Strace mode: captures semihosting output that goes to non-stdout fd
    # This is needed when QEMU's chardev fd isn't connected to the terminal
    STRACE_LOG="${WORK_DIR}/qemu_strace.txt"
    strace -f -e trace=write -o "${STRACE_LOG}" \
        timeout ${TIMEOUT} "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" "${TEST_ADX}" 2>&1 || true

    # Extract semihosting output from strace (writes to high fd numbers)
    echo ""
    echo "--- QEMU Output (via strace) ---"
    grep 'write([0-9]' "${STRACE_LOG}" | grep -v 'write(8,' | \
        sed -n 's/.*write([0-9]*, "\(.*\)", [0-9]*).*/\1/p' | \
        while IFS= read -r line; do
            echo -e "${line}"
        done
    echo "--- End ---"

    # Check pass/fail
    if grep -q '"accuracy checking pass' "${STRACE_LOG}"; then
        PASS_COUNT=$(grep -c '"accuracy checking pass' "${STRACE_LOG}")
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: PASS (${PASS_COUNT} sub-test(s) passed)"
        exit 0
    elif grep -q '"accuracy checking fail' "${STRACE_LOG}"; then
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: FAIL"
        exit 1
    else
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: UNKNOWN (no pass/fail found — QEMU may have timed out or crashed)"
        exit 2
    fi
else
    # Direct mode: works when terminal properly connects QEMU chardev
    OUTPUT=$(timeout ${TIMEOUT} "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" "${TEST_ADX}" 2>&1) || true
    echo "${OUTPUT}"

    if echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking pass"; then
        PASS_COUNT=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -c "accuracy checking pass")
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: PASS (${PASS_COUNT} sub-test(s) passed)"
        exit 0
    elif echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking fail"; then
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: FAIL"
        exit 1
    else
        echo ""
        echo "WARNING: No output from QEMU. Try --strace mode."
        exit 2
    fi
fi

################################################################################

#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --skip-build
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace
#
# Environment:
#   TOOLCHAIN_PATH  - Path to Andes toolchain bin/ (default: /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin)
#   QEMU_SYS_RV64   - Path to QEMU binary (default: /local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64)
################################################################################
set -euo pipefail

TOOLCHAIN_PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH:-/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin}"
QEMU_SYS_RV64="${QEMU_SYS_RV64:-/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VLEN=512
SKIP_BUILD=0
USE_STRACE=0
TIMEOUT=120
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
VEC_CFG="zve64d"

#===== Parse arguments =====
TEST_NAME="${1:-}"
if [ -z "${TEST_NAME}" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <test_name> [--vlen N] [--skip-build] [--strace] [--timeout N] [--cpu NAME]"
    echo ""
    echo "  test_name     Unit test name (without t_ prefix), e.g. conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any"
    echo "  --vlen N      Vector length: 128, 256, 512 (default), 1024"
    echo "  --skip-build  Skip library rebuild (use existing libnn.a)"
    echo "  --strace      Use strace to capture output (workaround for fd redirection issues)"
    echo "  --timeout N   QEMU timeout in seconds (default: 120)"
    echo "  --cpu NAME    CPU build target (default: ax45mpv)"
    exit 1
fi
shift

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --vlen)    VLEN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --skip-build) SKIP_BUILD=1; shift ;;
        --strace)  USE_STRACE=1; shift ;;
        --timeout) TIMEOUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cpu)     CPU_NAME="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        *) echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# FPGA Benchmark Script for libnn
#
# Builds libnn, compiles a single unit test, and runs it on the FPGA board
# to measure cycle-accurate performance and verify correctness.
#
# Usage:
#   ./run_fpga_test.sh <test_name> [options]
#
# Examples:
#   ./run_fpga_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
#   ./run_fpga_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --port 1117
#   ./run_fpga_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --skip-build --perf
#   ./run_fpga_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --tag baseline
#
# Environment:
#   TOOLCHAIN_PATH  - Path to Andes toolchain bin/ (default: /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin)
#   MYPC            - Board host (default: sw-boards.andestech.com)
#   MYPORT          - Board port (default: 1116)
################################################################################
set -euo pipefail

#===== Defaults =====
TOOLCHAIN_PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH:-/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin}"
export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"
SKIP_BUILD=0
PERF_ONLY=0
TAG=""
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
VEC_CFG="zve64d"

#===== Parse arguments =====
TEST_NAME="${1:-}"
if [ -z "${TEST_NAME}" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <test_name> [--port N] [--host H] [--skip-build] [--perf] [--tag NAME] [--cpu NAME]"
    echo ""
    echo "  test_name     Unit test name (without t_ prefix), e.g. conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any"
    echo "  --port N      FPGA board port (default: 1116)"
    echo "  --host H      FPGA board host (default: sw-boards.andestech.com)"
    echo "  --skip-build  Skip library rebuild (use existing libnn.a)"
    echo "  --perf        Performance-only mode: ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1, runs only the large test case"
    echo "  --tag NAME    Tag for output file naming (e.g. 'baseline', 'optimized')"
    echo "  --cpu NAME    CPU build target (default: ax45mpv)"
    exit 1
fi
shift

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --port)    export MYPORT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --host)    export MYPC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --skip-build) SKIP_BUILD=1; shift ;;
        --perf)    PERF_ONLY=1; shift ;;
        --tag)     TAG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cpu)     CPU_NAME="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        *) echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

#===== Resolve paths =====
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
PROJECT_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv"
UNIT_TEST_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
WORK_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/work"
BOARD_WRAPPER="${PROJECT_DIR}/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"

export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}:${LIB_ROOT}:${PATH}"
CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"

# Build output filename
if [ -n "${TAG}" ]; then
    ADX_NAME="t_${TEST_NAME}_${TAG}.adx"
else
    ADX_NAME="t_${TEST_NAME}.adx"
fi

echo "============================================"
echo "  FPGA Benchmark: t_${TEST_NAME}"
echo "============================================"
echo "  Board:      ${MYPC}:${MYPORT}"
echo "  CPU:        ${CPU_NAME}"
echo "  Toolchain:  ${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}"
echo "  Perf-only:  ${PERF_ONLY}"
echo "  Tag:        ${TAG:-<none>}"
echo "============================================"

#===== Step 1: Build library =====
if [ ${SKIP_BUILD} -eq 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "[1/3] Building libnn for ${CPU_NAME}..."
    LIB_CFLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER -fno-strict-aliasing -I./Include -I./internal -mzfh"
    cd "${LIB_ROOT}"
    bash build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}" > /dev/null 2>&1
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "  FAILED: Library build error. Re-run without stdout redirect to see errors:"
        echo "    cd ${LIB_ROOT} && bash build.sh ${CPU_NAME} \"${LIB_CFLAG}\" ${CC}"
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "  OK: libnn.a built"
else
    echo ""
    echo "[1/3] Skipping library build (--skip-build)"
    if [ ! -f "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" ]; then
        echo "  ERROR: libnn.a not found. Remove --skip-build."
        exit 1
    fi
fi

#===== Step 2: Compile test binary =====
echo ""
echo "[2/3] Compiling test binary..."
mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"

TEST_SRC="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.c"
TEST_ADX="${WORK_DIR}/${ADX_NAME}"

if [ ! -f "${TEST_SRC}" ]; then
    echo "  ERROR: Test source not found: ${TEST_SRC}"
    exit 1
fi

# Key flags for FPGA:
#   -mvh                   : enables semihosting (GDB handles file I/O on host side)
#   -DGETENV_TESTDIR=...   : absolute path to unit_func/ so binary finds bin/*.bin golden data
#   -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=N   : 1=perf mode (large test only), 0=full accuracy test
#   -DENA_RUN_TWICE        : run function twice for warm-cache measurement
${CC} -mvh \
    -Wall -Werror -O3 \
    -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 \
    -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER \
    -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mzfh \
    -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE \
    -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 \
    -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${PERF_ONLY} \
    -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0 \
    -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP \
    -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_VL4 \
    -DGETENV_TESTDIR="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}" \
    -I"${LIB_ROOT}/Include/" \
    -I"${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include/" \
    -I"${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/include/" \
    -o "${TEST_ADX}" \
    "${TEST_SRC}" \
    "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" -lm 2>&1

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "  FAILED: Compilation error"
    exit 1
fi
echo "  OK: ${ADX_NAME}"

#===== Step 3: Run on FPGA board =====
echo ""
echo "[3/3] Running on FPGA board ${MYPC}:${MYPORT}..."

# Clear first-reset flag so GDB script initializes the board properly
rm -f "${PROJECT_DIR}/First_Reset_done"

cd "${WORK_DIR}"
OUTPUT=$("${BOARD_WRAPPER}" "${TEST_ADX}" 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?

# Parse and display results
echo ""
echo "--- Board Output ---"
# Extract the useful lines (inst/cycle counts and pass/fail)
echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -E "inst count|cycle count|accuracy checking|Error" || true
echo "--- End ---"

# Extract cycle/inst counts
INST=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep "inst count" | tail -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+' | tail -1)
CYCLES=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep "cycle count" | tail -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+' | tail -1)

# Save result to log
LOG_FILE="${WORK_DIR}/${TEST_NAME}${TAG:+_${TAG}}.log"
{
    echo "test:   ${TEST_NAME}"
    echo "tag:    ${TAG:-none}"
    echo "date:   $(date -Iseconds)"
    echo "board:  ${MYPC}:${MYPORT}"
    echo "inst:   ${INST:-N/A}"
    echo "cycles: ${CYCLES:-N/A}"
    if echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking pass"; then
        echo "result: PASS"
    elif echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking fail"; then
        echo "result: FAIL"
    else
        echo "result: UNKNOWN"
    fi
} > "${LOG_FILE}"

echo ""
cat "${LOG_FILE}"
echo ""
echo "Log saved to: ${LOG_FILE}"

# Final status
if echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking pass"; then
    echo ""
    echo "RESULT: PASS  inst=${INST}  cycles=${CYCLES}"
    exit 0
elif echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking fail"; then
    echo ""
    echo "RESULT: FAIL  inst=${INST}  cycles=${CYCLES}"
    exit 1
else
    echo ""
    echo "RESULT: ERROR (board connection or execution failure)"
    echo ""
    echo "Full output:"
    echo "${OUTPUT}" | tail -20
    exit 2
fi

fi
shift

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --port)    export MYPORT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --host)    export MYPC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --skip-build) SKIP_BUILD=1; shift ;;
        --perf)    PERF_ONLY=1; shift ;;
        --tag)     TAG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cpu)     CPU_NAME="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        *) echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

#===== Resolve paths =====
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
PROJECT_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv"
UNIT_TEST_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
WORK_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/work"
BOARD_WRAPPER="${PROJECT_DIR}/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"

export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}:${LIB_ROOT}:${PATH}"
CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"

# Build output filename
if [ -n "${TAG}" ]; then
    ADX_NAME="t_${TEST_NAME}_${TAG}.adx"
else
    ADX_NAME="t_${TEST_NAME}.adx"
fi

echo "============================================"
echo "  FPGA Benchmark: t_${TEST_NAME}"
echo "============================================"
echo "  Board:      ${MYPC}:${MYPORT}"
echo "  CPU:        ${CPU_NAME}"
echo "  Toolchain:  ${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}"
echo "  Perf-only:  ${PERF_ONLY}"
echo "  Tag:        ${TAG:-<none>}"
echo "============================================"

#===== Step 1: Build library =====
if [ ${SKIP_BUILD} -eq 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "[1/3] Building libnn for ${CPU_NAME}..."
    LIB_CFLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER -fno-strict-aliasing -I./Include -I./internal -mzfh"
    cd "${LIB_ROOT}"
    bash build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}" > /dev/null 2>&1
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "  FAILED: Library build error. Re-run without stdout redirect to see errors:"
        echo "    cd ${LIB_ROOT} && bash build.sh ${CPU_NAME} \"${LIB_CFLAG}\" ${CC}"
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "  OK: libnn.a built"
else
    echo ""
    echo "[1/3] Skipping library build (--skip-build)"
    if [ ! -f "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" ]; then
        echo "  ERROR: libnn.a not found. Remove --skip-build."
        exit 1
    fi
fi

#===== Step 2: Compile test binary =====
echo ""
echo "[2/3] Compiling test binary..."
mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"

TEST_SRC="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.c"
TEST_ADX="${WORK_DIR}/${ADX_NAME}"

if [ ! -f "${TEST_SRC}" ]; then
    echo "  ERROR: Test source not found: ${TEST_SRC}"
    exit 1
fi

# Key flags for FPGA:
#   -mvh                   : enables semihosting (GDB handles file I/O on host side)
#   -DGETENV_TESTDIR=...   : absolute path to unit_func/ so binary finds bin/*.bin golden data
#   -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=N   : 1=perf mode (large test only), 0=full accuracy test
#   -DENA_RUN_TWICE        : run function twice for warm-cache measurement
${CC} -mvh \
    -Wall -Werror -O3 \
    -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 \
    -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER \
    -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mzfh \
    -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE \
    -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 \
    -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${PERF_ONLY} \
    -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0 \
    -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP \
    -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_VL4 \
    -DGETENV_TESTDIR="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}" \
    -I"${LIB_ROOT}/Include/" \
    -I"${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include/" \
    -I"${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/include/" \
    -o "${TEST_ADX}" \
    "${TEST_SRC}" \
    "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" -lm 2>&1

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "  FAILED: Compilation error"
    exit 1
fi
echo "  OK: ${ADX_NAME}"

#===== Step 3: Run on FPGA board =====
echo ""
echo "[3/3] Running on FPGA board ${MYPC}:${MYPORT}..."

# Clear first-reset flag so GDB script initializes the board properly
rm -f "${PROJECT_DIR}/First_Reset_done"

cd "${WORK_DIR}"
OUTPUT=$("${BOARD_WRAPPER}" "${TEST_ADX}" 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?

# Parse and display results
echo ""
echo "--- Board Output ---"
# Extract the useful lines (inst/cycle counts and pass/fail)
echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -E "inst count|cycle count|accuracy checking|Error" || true
echo "--- End ---"

# Extract cycle/inst counts
INST=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep "inst count" | tail -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+' | tail -1)
CYCLES=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep "cycle count" | tail -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+' | tail -1)

# Save result to log
LOG_FILE="${WORK_DIR}/${TEST_NAME}${TAG:+_${TAG}}.log"
{
    echo "test:   ${TEST_NAME}"
    echo "tag:    ${TAG:-none}"
    echo "date:   $(date -Iseconds)"
    echo "board:  ${MYPC}:${MYPORT}"
    echo "inst:   ${INST:-N/A}"
    echo "cycles: ${CYCLES:-N/A}"
    if echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking pass"; then
        echo "result: PASS"
    elif echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking fail"; then
        echo "result: FAIL"
    else
        echo "result: UNKNOWN"
    fi
} > "${LOG_FILE}"

echo ""
cat "${LOG_FILE}"
echo ""
echo "Log saved to: ${LOG_FILE}"

# Final status
if echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking pass"; then
    echo ""
    echo "RESULT: PASS  inst=${INST}  cycles=${CYCLES}"
    exit 0
elif echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking fail"; then
    echo ""
    echo "RESULT: FAIL  inst=${INST}  cycles=${CYCLES}"
    exit 1
else
    echo ""
    echo "RESULT: ERROR (board connection or execution failure)"
    echo ""
    echo "Full output:"
    echo "${OUTPUT}" | tail -20
    exit 2
fi

content.text:
Both scripts work. Here's the summary:

**`qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh`** — QEMU functional validation
```bash
./qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace
./qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --skip-build --vlen 1024
```

**`qemu-validate/run_fpga_test.sh`** — FPGA cycle-accurate benchmark
```bash
./qemu-validate/run_fpga_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --perf --tag baseline --port 1116
./qemu-validate/run_fpga_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --perf --tag optimized --skip-build
```

Key parameters:
- `--skip-build` — reuse existing `libnn.a` (saves rebuild time)
- `--strace` — captures QEMU output via strace (needed in Claude Code sandbox)
- `--perf` — runs only the large test case for performance measurement
- `--tag NAME` — labels the output log for A/B comparison
- `--port N` / `--vlen N` — board port / vector length

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# QEMU Validation Script for libnn
#
# Builds libnn, compiles a single unit test, and runs it on QEMU to verify
# correctness against golden data.
#
# Usage:
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> [options]
#
# Examples:
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --vlen 1024
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --skip-build
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace
#
# Environment:
#   TOOLCHAIN_PATH  - Path to Andes toolchain bin/ (default: /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin)
#   QEMU_SYS_RV64   - Path to QEMU binary (default: /local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64)
################################################################################
set -euo pipefail

#===== Defaults =====
TOOLCHAIN_PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH:-/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin}"
QEMU_SYS_RV64="${QEMU_SYS_RV64:-/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VLEN=512
SKIP_BUILD=0
USE_STRACE=0
TIMEOUT=120
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
VEC_CFG="zve64d"

#===== Parse arguments =====
TEST_NAME="${1:-}"
if [ -z "${TEST_NAME}" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <test_name> [--vlen N] [--skip-build] [--strace] [--timeout N] [--cpu NAME]"
    echo ""
    echo "  test_name     Unit test name (without t_ prefix), e.g. conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any"
    echo "  --vlen N      Vector length: 128, 256, 512 (default), 1024"
    echo "  --skip-build  Skip library rebuild (use existing libnn.a)"
    echo "  --strace      Use strace to capture output (workaround for fd redirection issues)"
    echo "  --timeout N   QEMU timeout in seconds (default: 120)"
    echo "  --cpu NAME    CPU build target (default: ax45mpv)"
    exit 1
fi
shift

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --vlen)    VLEN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --skip-build) SKIP_BUILD=1; shift ;;
        --strace)  USE_STRACE=1; shift ;;
        --timeout) TIMEOUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cpu)     CPU_NAME="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        *) echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

#===== Resolve paths =====
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
PROJECT_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv"
UNIT_TEST_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
WORK_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/work"

export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}:${LIB_ROOT}:${PATH}"
CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"

# Select QEMU wrapper based on VLEN
QEMU_WRAPPER="${PROJECT_DIR}/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v${VLEN}_d${VLEN}_b512"
if [ ! -f "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" ]; then
    # Try common fallback patterns
    QEMU_WRAPPER="${PROJECT_DIR}/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v${VLEN}_d$((VLEN/2))_b512"
fi
if [ ! -f "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" ]; then
    echo "Error: No QEMU wrapper found for VLEN=${VLEN} in ${PROJECT_DIR}"
    ls "${PROJECT_DIR}"/riscv-qemu-wrapper-* 2>/dev/null
    exit 1
fi

echo "============================================"
echo "  QEMU Validation: t_${TEST_NAME}"
echo "============================================"
echo "  VLEN:       ${VLEN}"
echo "  CPU:        ${CPU_NAME}"
echo "  Toolchain:  ${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}"
echo "  QEMU:       ${QEMU_SYS_RV64}"
echo "  Wrapper:    $(basename ${QEMU_WRAPPER})"
echo "  Strace:     ${USE_STRACE}"
echo "============================================"

#===== Step 1: Build library =====
if [ ${SKIP_BUILD} -eq 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "[1/3] Building libnn for ${CPU_NAME}..."
    LIB_CFLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER -fno-strict-aliasing -I./Include -I./internal -mzfh"
    cd "${LIB_ROOT}"
    bash build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}" > /dev/null 2>&1
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "  FAILED: Library build error"
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "  OK: libnn.a built"
else
    echo ""
    echo "[1/3] Skipping library build (--skip-build)"
    if [ ! -f "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" ]; then
        echo "  ERROR: libnn.a not found. Remove --skip-build."
        exit 1
    fi
fi

#===== Step 2: Compile test binary =====
echo ""
echo "[2/3] Compiling test binary..."
mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"

TEST_SRC="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.c"
TEST_ADX="${WORK_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.adx"

if [ ! -f "${TEST_SRC}" ]; then
    echo "  ERROR: Test source not found: ${TEST_SRC}"
    exit 1
fi

${CC} -mvh \
    -Wall -Werror -O3 \
    -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 \
    -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER \
    -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mzfh \
    -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE \
    -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=0 -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0 \
    -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -DENA_VL4 \
    -I"${LIB_ROOT}/Include/" \
    -I"${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include/" \
    -I"${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/include/" \
    -o "${TEST_ADX}" \
    "${TEST_SRC}" \
    "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" -lm 2>&1

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "  FAILED: Compilation error"
    exit 1
fi
echo "  OK: $(basename ${TEST_ADX})"

#===== Step 3: Run on QEMU =====
echo ""
echo "[3/3] Running on QEMU (timeout=${TIMEOUT}s)..."

export QEMU_SYS_RV64
export VEC_CFG

cd "${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"

if [ ${USE_STRACE} -eq 1 ]; then
    # Strace mode: captures semihosting output that goes to non-stdout fd
    # This is needed when QEMU's chardev fd isn't connected to the terminal
    STRACE_LOG="${WORK_DIR}/qemu_strace.txt"
    strace -f -e trace=write -o "${STRACE_LOG}" \
        timeout ${TIMEOUT} "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" "${TEST_ADX}" 2>&1 || true

    # Extract semihosting output from strace (writes to high fd numbers)
    echo ""
    echo "--- QEMU Output (via strace) ---"
    grep 'write([0-9]' "${STRACE_LOG}" | grep -v 'write(8,' | \
        sed -n 's/.*write([0-9]*, "\(.*\)", [0-9]*).*/\1/p' | \
        while IFS= read -r line; do
            echo -e "${line}"
        done
    echo "--- End ---"

    # Check pass/fail
    if grep -q '"accuracy checking pass' "${STRACE_LOG}"; then
        PASS_COUNT=$(grep -c '"accuracy checking pass' "${STRACE_LOG}")
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: PASS (${PASS_COUNT} sub-test(s) passed)"
        exit 0
    elif grep -q '"accuracy checking fail' "${STRACE_LOG}"; then
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: FAIL"
        exit 1
    else
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: UNKNOWN (no pass/fail found — QEMU may have timed out or crashed)"
        exit 2
    fi
else
    # Direct mode: works when terminal properly connects QEMU chardev
    OUTPUT=$(timeout ${TIMEOUT} "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" "${TEST_ADX}" 2>&1) || true
    echo "${OUTPUT}"

    if echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking pass"; then
        PASS_COUNT=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -c "accuracy checking pass")
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: PASS (${PASS_COUNT} sub-test(s) passed)"
        exit 0
    elif echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking fail"; then
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: FAIL"
        exit 1
    else
        echo ""
        echo "WARNING: No output from QEMU. Try --strace mode."
        exit 2
    fi
fi

################################################################################
# Build libnn for a specific CPU target
#
# Usage:
#   ./build_libnn.sh [options]
#
# Examples:
#   ./build_libnn.sh
#   ./build_libnn.sh --cpu ax45mpv --vec-cfg zve64d
#   ./build_libnn.sh --verbose
#
# Environment:
#   TOOLCHAIN_PATH  - Path to Andes toolchain bin/ (default: /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin)
################################################################################
set -euo pipefail

#===== Defaults =====
TOOLCHAIN_PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH:-/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin}"
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
VEC_CFG="zve64d"
VERBOSE=0

#===== Parse arguments =====
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --cpu)     CPU_NAME="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --vec-cfg) VEC_CFG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --verbose) VERBOSE=1; shift ;;
        -h|--help)
            echo "Usage: $0 [--cpu NAME] [--vec-cfg CFG] [--verbose]"
            echo ""
            echo "  --cpu NAME     CPU build target (default: ax45mpv)"
            echo "  --vec-cfg CFG  Vector config: zve32x, zve32f, zve64x, zve64f, zve64d (default: zve64d)"
            echo "  --verbose      Show full build output"
            exit 0
            ;;

echo "Building libnn for ${CPU_NAME} (${VEC_CFG})..."
cd "${LIB_ROOT}"

if [ ${VERBOSE} -eq 1 ]; then
    bash build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}" 2>&1
else
    bash build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}" > /dev/null 2>&1
fi

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "FAILED: Library build error. Re-run with --verbose to see details."
    exit 1
fi

echo "OK: ${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a"
ls -lh "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a"
